_meta.description: Absorption band catalog for CRISM spectral range (1.0-3.9um). Sources: Clark et al. 1990, Viviano-Beck et al. 2014, Bishop et al. 2008, USGS splib07.

OH_stretch_overtone.cause: First overtone of OH stretching vibration (2v_OH)
OH_stretch_overtone.notes: Position shifts with cation: Mg-OH ~1.39um, Al-OH ~1.41um, Prehnite ~1.48um

H2O_combination.cause: Combination of H2O bending + OH stretch (v2+v3)
H2O_combination.notes: Absent or very weak in anhydrous minerals (olivine, pyroxene, hematite). Key hydration indicator.

H2O_monohydrated_shifted.cause: H2O combination shifted by crystal structure with single structural water
H2O_monohydrated_shifted.notes: Diagnostic shift from 1.9um polyhydrated to 2.1um monohydrated position

Al_OH_combination.notes: Kaolinite uniquely shows doublet; others show single band. Position distinguishes Al-phyllosilicates from Fe/Mg-phyllosilicates.

Fe_OH_combination.notes: Key discriminator between Fe and Mg smectites. ~20nm shift from Mg-OH.

Mg_OH_combination.notes: Shifted redward from Fe-OH by ~20nm due to heavier Mg-O bond.

Metal_OH_overtone.notes: Less diagnostic alone but confirms phyllosilicate presence when paired with 2.2-2.3um bands.

CO3_combination_1.notes: Overlaps with Mg-OH region. Must check for paired 2.5um band to confirm carbonate vs phyllosilicate.

CO3_combination_2.notes: Paired with 2.3um CO3 band. Presence of BOTH 2.3+2.5um confirms carbonate; 2.3um alone could be phyllosilicate.

H2O_fundamental.notes: Very strong, broad absorption. Saturates in water ice. Diagnostic for goethite (vs anhydrous hematite).

CO3_v3_antisymmetric.notes: Strong carbonate indicator. Can confirm carbonate identification from 2.3+2.5um bands.

Fe2_crystal_field_band1.cause: Fe2+ electronic transition in M2 crystallographic site
Fe2_crystal_field_band1.notes: Within CRISM IR range. Broad in olivine, narrower in pyroxene. Band I for pyroxene classification.

Fe2_crystal_field_band2_LCP.cause: Fe2+ electronic transition in M1 site (low-Ca pyroxene)
Fe2_crystal_field_band2_LCP.notes: Band II position is primary LCP vs HCP discriminator. LCP ~1.83um.

Fe2_crystal_field_band2_HCP.cause: Fe2+ electronic transition in M1 site (high-Ca pyroxene)
Fe2_crystal_field_band2_HCP.notes: Band II position shifted redward by Ca in M2 site. HCP ~2.15um. ~300nm shift from LCP.

Serpentine_unique.cause: Mg-OH combination unique to serpentine structure
Serpentine_unique.notes: Diagnostic band not seen in other Mg-phyllosilicates. Key discriminator from saponite.

Gypsum_specific.cause: H2O combination specific to gypsum crystal structure
Gypsum_specific.notes: Unique to gypsum among sulfates. Not seen in bassanite, kieserite, or polyhydrated sulfates.

BD2100_2.high_value_means: Shifted H2O band at ~2.1um indicating monohydrated phase

D2300.high_value_means: Broad 2.3um drop indicating Metal-OH bearing phase

OLINDEX3.high_value_means: Broad 1um complex absorption from Fe2+ in olivine

decision_rules.phyllosilicate_vs_carbonate: Both show ~2.3um absorption. Carbonate has paired 2.3+2.5um bands and 3.4um CO3 feature. Phyllosilicate has 1.9um H2O and lacks 2.5um paired band.
decision_rules.Fe_vs_Mg_smectite: Fe-OH at 2.28-2.29um vs Mg-OH at 2.31-2.32um. ~20-30nm shift is diagnostic.
decision_rules.Fe_Mg_vs_Al_phyllosilicate: Fe/Mg-OH at 2.28-2.32um vs Al-OH at 2.20-2.21um. ~100nm shift.
decision_rules.LCP_vs_HCP: Band II position: LCP ~1.83um vs HCP ~2.15um. ~300nm shift correlates with Ca content.
decision_rules.olivine_vs_pyroxene: Olivine: single broad 1um complex. Pyroxene: two distinct bands at 1.0 and 1.8-2.2um.
decision_rules.monohydrated_vs_polyhydrated_sulfate: Monohydrated (kieserite): H2O shifted to 2.07-2.13um. Polyhydrated: H2O at 1.93um.
decision_rules.kaolinite_vs_montmorillonite: Kaolinite: doublet at 2.16+2.21um. Montmorillonite: single band at 2.21um.
decision_rules.gypsum_vs_other_sulfate: Gypsum: unique 1.75um band and 2.2um triplet. Other sulfates lack these.
decision_rules.serpentine_vs_saponite: Serpentine: unique 2.11um band, weak/absent 1.9um. Saponite: no 2.11um, strong 1.9um.
